spec_match.rationale: Page runs script and renders dynamic UI, but the visible API surface (window., document.querySelector, PaymentRequest) only partially mirrors what the feature summary calls out — the headline behaviour isn't unambiguously demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (6 buttons / 6 inputs / 1 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: No matching demo-card blurb on the feature index for this concept slug (link probably uses a different label).

edge_cases.rationale: Page references 6 edge case / failure / fallback hits and the control surface has room to exercise distinct branches, so the happy path is not the only one shown.

references.rationale: Page does not include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link — violates the rule #3 self-heal invariant.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 110 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the link between the demo and the spec's headline behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Confirm the on-screen interaction maps to a concrete API call from the feature summary on chromestatus: "Adds a new static method to the Payment Request that allows web developers to get the capabilities of the browser's implementation of Secure Payment Confirmation.  This helps web developers to easily ". If the call is being shimmed or simulated, surface that fact in the page and add a real invocation behind a feature-detect.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Cross-check the feature index demo-card label
openQuestions.detail: The feature index demo-card for slug `checkout-simulator` could not be matched. Either the index needs a card pointing here, or the slug needs to be corrected on one side.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link
openQuestions.detail: Page is missing the chromestatus.com/feature/4727235745546240 link inside a `references` section. CLAUDE.md rule #3 requires this on every concept so the slug-recovery script can re-derive the canonical name.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Adds a new static method to the Payment Request that allows web developers to get the capabilities of the browser's implementation of Secure Payment Confirmation.  This helps web developers to easily know what capabilities are available for") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://w3c.github.io/secure-payment-confirmation/#sctn-secure-payment-confirmation-capabilities to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~6144 chars of script across 6 button(s) and 7 other control(s). hits 2 pass / 2 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
